Description: This column represents the highest level of financial categorization, capturing broad financial categories such as Total Cost, Income, Non-Group Assets, Non-Group Liabilities, and Capital. It serves as the primary classification for understanding the overall financial structure and performance of the organization.

This column breaks down the broad categories from ACCOUNT (L0) into more specific subcategories, such as Directly Controllable Costs, Service Charge, Funded Liabilities, and PE Assets. It provides a more detailed view of financial activities, helping to identify specific areas of financial management and control.

This column further refines the subcategories from ACCOUNT (L1) into even more specific financial elements, including items like Non Staff Costs, Staff Costs exc Bonus, Non-Grp Liabs Other, and PE Liabilities. It offers insights into particular financial transactions and obligations, crucial for detailed financial analysis and reporting.

This column provides a deeper level of detail, focusing on specific financial transactions or activities within the subcategories from ACCOUNT (L2). It includes items such as Marketing, Adv & Sponsor, Accrued Income, Derivative Assets, and Loans to Banks, allowing for precise tracking and management of financial operations.

This column offers an even finer granularity of financial data, detailing specific components or activities within the categories from ACCOUNT (L3). It includes items like Advertising, Acquired Intangibles, Premises, and Goodwill & Intangibles, which are critical for understanding the exact nature of financial transactions.

This column represents the most detailed level of financial categorization, focusing on individual financial elements or transactions within the categories from ACCOUNT (L4). It includes items like Allowance, Audit Fees, Benefits - Car, and Capitalised Software, providing the most precise financial data available, essential for detailed financial audits and compliance checks.
A member which is most granular across the level in the hierarchy

This column categorizes data according to different business views, such as Corporate and Institutional Banking (CIB), Wealth and Retail Banking (WRB), and Financial Planning and Analysis (FPNA). It enables users to analyze data from various perspectives, aligning with specific business needs and strategies.
Primary key for this dimension. Its defined by concatenating of all other attributes in the dimension
